name: Lippia Fever Tea
description: An exceptionally aromatic sub-shrub widely used across sub-Saharan Africa. The leaves are packed with potent essential oils (carvacrol, myrcene, and linalool) showing clinical acaricidal efficacy for scabies mites and antipyretic properties for viral rigors.
preparation: Fever infusion: Steep 5g of dried leaves in 300ml of boiling water for 10 minutes. Drink warm. For Scabies: Crush fresh aromatic leaves directly and rub vigorously onto affected itchy skin patches twice daily.
safetyAlert: Extremely safe for topical and internal tea preparations. Mild skin redness may occur in highly sensitive individuals if applied raw.
interactions: None known. Safe to combine with clinical supportive hydration.
